summary: Fair‑quality soundboard hampered by static, yet the 1993 Chicago show dazzles with a blistering Deal and soaring China > Rider.
review: The performance is widely praised – fans point to a scorching Deal, an epic China > Rider opening the second set, and emotionally charged moments like Broken Arrow and the Rain encore. Recording-wise the SBD suffers from pervasive static and crackle that some listeners find distracting, though the overall mix remains listenable. Musical highlights outweigh technical flaws, making it a memorable summer‑'93 experience for most attendees.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise (Deal, China>Rider, Broken Arrow) pushes the rating upward, but repeated complaints about static and crackle lower it; overall score sits just below a solid 4‑star level.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Static throughout the show; noticeable crackle/hiss during Deal and China>Rider; otherwise the mix is audible but not crisp.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as upbeat but a bit short; second set launches with China>Rider and maintains high energy, ending on a rainy encore that left many fans emotionally satisfied.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His finger‑picked solo on China>Rider is called "amazing" and he “shreds” throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for wild riffs during China>Rider and strong playing overall; some note his “wild Phil riffs”.
